member.name: Sir Andrew Mitchell
member.fullTitle: Rt Hon Sir Andrew Mitchell MP

addresses: Sutton Coldfield Conservative Association, 36B High Street, Royal Sutton Coldfield, B72 1UP

member.synopsis: The Rt Hon Sir Andrew Mitchell is the Conservative MP for Sutton Coldfield , and has been an MP continually since 7 June 2001.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Water: Sutton Park
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what discussions her Department has had with Birmingham City Council on improving water safety at Sutton Park.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Responsibility for water safety and drowning prevention is shared across a range of organisations, including local authorities, emergency services, regulators, education partners and landowners. Defra's role primarily relates to the water environment and ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Water: Sutton Park
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what steps her Department is taking to help improve water safety measures at Sutton Park.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Responsibility for water safety and drowning prevention is shared across a range of organisations, including local authorities, emergency services, regulators, education partners and landowners. Defra's role primarily relates to the water environment and ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: West Midlands Police: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, if she will provide additional funding for West Midlands Police to help address low level crime during the summer months.
writtenQuestions.answerText: West Midlands Police will receive up to £883.9 million in 2026-27, an increase of up to £36.9 million when compared to 2025-26.Decisions on how funding and resources are utilised are an operational matter for Chief Constables and locally elected Police an...

writtenQuestions.heading: University Hospitals Birmingham NHS Foundation Trust: Agency Workers
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, how much University Hospitals Birmingham NHS Foundation Trust spent on agency staff in each of the last five years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The following table shows the University Hospitals Birmingham NHS Foundation Trust’s spend on agency/contract staff since 2021/22, according to University Hospitals Birmingham NHS Foundation Trust’s last five published annual reports and accounts:YearActu...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: West Midlands Police: Training
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, whether she is taking steps to increase funding for ongoing training in West Midlands Police to support career progression for local officers.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This Government is committed to ensuring there are consistent, high standards in policing. That is why, since being elected, the government has increased funding to forces by £2 billion. West Midlands Police will receive up to £883.9 million in 2026-27, a...

writtenQuestions.heading: Electric Vehicles: Charging Points
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what assessment she has made of the potential impact of the availability of public electric vehicle charging infrastructure on consumer uptake of electric vehicles in Royal Sutton Coldfield.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As of 1 April 2026, there were 102 public EV chargers in Sutton Coldfield. To build on this, the West Midlands Combined Authority has received over £18 million to deliver additional local charging infrastructure through the Local EV Infrastructure Fund.

writtenQuestions.heading: Roads: Birmingham
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, how much additional funding has been allocated to Birmingham City Council from the £7.3 billion the Government has announced to support local highways maintenance.
writtenQuestions.answerText: For this financial year, West Midlands Combined Authority will receive an additional £17 million in their Integrated Settlement to fund highways maintenance in Birmingham. This amount was calculated using the Department’s local highways maintenance fundin...

writtenQuestions.heading: Hospitality Industry: Sutton Coldfield
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what recent assessment she has made of the impact of the increase in (a) employer National Insurance contributions and (b) the National Minimum Wage on hospitality businesses in Royal Sutton Coldfield.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises hospitality as a vital foundation sector, supporting millions of jobs, driving local economies (including in Royal Sutton Coldfield) and sustaining high streets and communities across the UK. The Government has taken a number of ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Probate: Standards
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, what the current average processing time is for probate applications.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Ministry of Justice publishes regular data on probate grant volumes, timeliness and open caseload in our quarterly Family Court statistics bulletin: Family Court Statistics Quarterly - GOV.UK

writtenQuestions.heading: Water Supply: West Midlands
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what recent assessment she has made of the level of the demand on water resources in the West Midlands; and what are the principal contributors to that demand.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The level of demand on water resources is currently very high in the West Midlands. South Staffs Water and Severn Trent Water, whose supply areas both include the West Midlands, are classed as seriously water-stressed in the Environment Agency’s 2021 wate...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Business: West Midlands
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what recent steps he has taken to encourage business investment in (a) Royal Sutton Coldfield and (b) the West Midlands.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is supporting business investment in Royal Sutton Coldfield and the wider West Midlands region through close engagement with local partners, including the West Midlands Combined Authority, to promote investment opportunities and strengthen ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Employment Schemes: Sutton Coldfield
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, if he will estimate the number of people that will benefit from Sector-based Work Academy Programmes in Royal Sutton Coldfield over the next 5 years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Sector Based Work Academy Programmes (SWAPs) are a successful employment support offer through which participants gain the skills and experience needed to move into work. Nearly 100,000 SWAPs were delivered in 2025/26, an increase of almost 15,000 on the ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Council Tax: Birmingham
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, whether guidance has been provided to Birmingham City Council in relation to advertising the range of council tax reductions that are available to local residents.
writtenQuestions.answerText: While guidance has not been issued specifically to Birmingham City Council in relation to advertising council tax reductions to local residents, the government recently issued an information letter to councils (here) to remind of the need to make council ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Primary Education: Mental Health Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, how many primary schools in Royal Sutton Coldfield have access to mental health support teams.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department is providing access to specialist mental health professionals in every school by expanding Mental Health Support Teams (MHSTs). Rollout is delivered with the NHS through Integrated Care Systems, prioritising areas based on need, disadvantag...

writtenQuestions.heading: Broadband: West Midlands
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what assessment her Department has made of the adequacy of digital connectivity for businesses in the West Midlands.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises that high quality digital connectivity is essential for businesses in the West Midlands and across the whole of the UK. Our ambition is for all populated areas to have access to higher quality standalone 5G by 2030 and we have a ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Zimbabwe: Commonwealth
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, what recent progress she has taken to assist Zimbabwe's return to the Commonwealth; and for what reason her Department vetoed the proposal that Zimbabwe should return at the
writtenQuestions.answerText: As I stated in my response of 2 February in response to the Rt Hon Member's Question 109061, the UK would welcome a Commonwealth assessment mission returning to Zimbabwe in due course to follow up on its recommendations from its last visit and the subsequ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Schools: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what assessment she has made of the potential impact of non-staffing costs on the sustainability of schools in Sutton Coldfield constituency.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department has published the Schools’ Costs Technical Note, outlining its national-level affordability forecasts and including data on non-staff expenditure, such as energy. This year’s publication and historical editions are available at: https://www...

writtenQuestions.heading: University Hospitals Birmingham NHS Foundation Trust: Batteries and Solar Power
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, whether University Hospitals Birmingham NHS Foundation Trust will receive funding for new batteries and solar panels as part of the Clean Energy Upgrades for Hospitals and Military Sites sche
writtenQuestions.answerText: In 25/26 the Great British Energy Solar scheme invested £130m in solar and battery storage projects across NHS trusts in England. This included funding for two projects at University Hospitals Birmingham NHS Foundation Trust. In 2026/27, a further £25m is...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Economic Crime: Conferences
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, for what reason the conference on illicit finance has been postponed.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As confirmed in the statement below, published on 22 May, the Illicit Finance Summit has been rescheduled to December to enable the broadest possible participation.https://www.gov.uk/government/news/uk-to-host-illicit-finance-summit-in-december.

writtenQuestions.heading: Tourism: West Midlands
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what steps she is taking to promote (a) Royal Sutton Coldfield and (b) the West Midlands as destinations for British holidaymakers in summer 2026.
writtenQuestions.answerText: DCMS recognises the contribution of the West Midlands, and the constituency of Sutton Coldfield, to the visitor economy. The upcoming summer 2026 season represents a milestone moment for the region. Birmingham’s hosting of the 2026 European Athletics Cham...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

Name of donor: Doha Forum Address of donor: Ministry of Foreign Affairs, Almirqab Tower, West Bay, Doha Qatar Estimate of the probable value (or amount of any donation): Flights (£5555), accommodation and hospitality (£402), value £5,957 Destination of visit: Qatar (Doha) Dates of visit: 5 December 2025 to 7 December 2025 Purpose of visit: To participate in the Doha Forum 2025 on diplomacy, dialogue and diversity, including meetings with fellow cross-party parliamentarians, political representations of other countries and Qatari ministers to discuss Qatar's role in seeking to resolve current regional conflicts. (Registered 22 December 2025)

Name of donor: Global Health Partnerships (formerly THET) Address of donor: 86 - 90 Paul Street, London EC2A 4NE Estimate of the probable value (or amount of any donation): Flights (£2,652.78), accommodation (£870.00), local transport (£730.00) and estimated value of meals and hospitality (£260.00), for myself and an International Development Advisor, value £4,512.78 Destination of visit: Zimbabwe (Harare) Dates of visit: 21 February 2026 to 25 February 2026 Purpose of visit: High-level meetings with Government of Zimbabwe representatives and health system stakeholders, site visit to project funded through the UK-Zimbabwe Health Partnership Pilot at Sally Mugabe Hospital, and attendance at UK-Zimbabwe Health Partnership Pilot celebration event hosted by the British Ambassador to Zimbabwe. (Registered 4 March 2026)

Name of donor: (1) Peter Harf (2) Konrad Adenauer Synergy Grant, “Human Security and Global Governance in the long 1990s”, Nuffield College, University of Oxford Address of donor: (1) private (2) Konrad Adenauer Synergy Grant, “Human Security and Global Governance in the long 1990s” Nuffield College, Oxford, OX1 1NF Estimate of the probable value (or amount of any donation): (1) For myself, accommodation (£3,599.13), local transport (£86.35) and meals and hospitality (£863.10). For an international development adviser, accommodation (£3,599.13), local transport (£86.35) and meals and hospitality (£863.10), value £9,097.16 (2) £320.12 for myself, return flights (£160.06). For an international development adviser, return flights (£160.06) Destination of visit: Italy (Venice) Dates of visit: 17 April 2026 to 20 April 2026 Purpose of visit: To attend a meeting convened by the Konrad Adenauer Synergy Research Grant, which is funded by the Alfred-Landecker Foundation, together with the Centre for Advanced Studies in International History and Law at the University of Cologne, and Nuffield College, Oxford, to discuss "Global Governance and Human Security in the 1990s" (a new Konrad Adenauer Synergy Research Grant awarded by the Foundation) (Registered 14 May 2026)

Name of donor: African Development Bank, Avenue Lamblin, Abidjan, Cote d'Ivoire Senior adviser to the African Development Bank, Avenue Lamblin, Abidjan, Cote d'Ivoire. I expect to devote 5 days a year to this role. I have waived the fee and will receive a nominal payment of £1. Date interest arose: 7 April 2025 Date interest ended: 1 September 2025 (Registered 8 April 2025; updated 14 September 2025)

Member of the Advisory Board convened by the Konrad Adenauer Synergy Research Grant, which is funded by the Alfred-Landecker Foundation, together with the Centre for Advanced Studies in International History and Law at the University of Cologne, and Nuffield College, Oxford, to facilitate research into Global Governance and Human Security in the 1990s. The project is being funded by a Konrad Adenauer Synergy Research Grant. This is an unpaid role. Date interest arose: 17 April 2026 (Registered 14 May 2026)
